nodes.title: être — to be
nodes.explanation: Être — to be — is the big one. You already know c'est from day one. Now just add the person: j'suis, t'es, il est, on est — hear how short they are? The past is easy too: j'ai été, like 'I have been'. You've got this.
nodes.street_notes: je suis → j'suis, often chuis in fast speech; tu es → t'es (near-universal); c'est carries most of the load in real speech.

drills.english: I'm at the café.
drills.french_formal: Je suis au café.

drills.english: Are you tired?
drills.french_formal: Tu es fatigué ?

drills.english: He's not here.
drills.french_formal: Il n'est pas là.

drills.english: We're ready to go.
drills.french_formal: Nous sommes prêts à partir.

drills.french_formal: Vous êtes au restaurant ?

drills.french_formal: Ils ne sont pas normaux.

drills.english: I've been at work all day.
drills.french_formal: J'ai été au travail toute la journée.
drills.french_street: J'ai été au taf toute la journée.

drills.english: Have you been to Paris?
drills.french_formal: Tu as été à Paris ?

drills.english: It was possible — we found the solution.
drills.french_formal: C'était possible — on a trouvé la solution.
drills.french_street: C'était possible — on a trouvé la solution.

drills.english: They were at the café last night.
drills.french_formal: Ils ont été au café hier soir.
drills.french_street: Ils ont été au café hier soir.

drills.english: I was tired.
drills.french_formal: J'étais fatigué.

drills.english: You were always at the café.
drills.french_formal: Tu étais toujours au café.

drills.english: It wasn't possible.

drills.french_street: On était dans le métro quand t'as appelé.

nodes.title: avoir — to have
nodes.explanation: Avoir — to have. Three sounds cover the whole present: 'ay' for j'ai, 'ah' for tu/il/on, 'avay' for vous avez. Street tip: tu as becomes t'as, almost always. The passé composé? That's just avoir plus eu — j'ai eu, t'as eu. You've already got the engine.
nodes.street_notes: tu as → t'as (near-universal); il y a → y'a. j'ai stays j'ai.

drills.english: I have a coffee.
drills.french_formal: J'ai un café.

drills.english: You have the solution.
drills.french_formal: Tu as la solution.

drills.english: He has a problem.
drills.french_formal: Il a un problème.

drills.english: We have time.
drills.french_formal: Nous avons le temps.

drills.english: You don't have the ticket.
drills.french_formal: Tu n'as pas le billet.

drills.english: There's a café here.
drills.french_formal: Il y a un café ici.

drills.english: I had a coffee this morning.
drills.french_formal: J'ai eu un café ce matin.
drills.french_street: J'ai eu un café ce matin.

drills.english: Did you have a problem?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu as eu un problème ?

drills.english: We had a great time.
drills.french_formal: Nous avons eu un super moment.
drills.french_street: On a eu un super moment.

drills.english: They didn't have a solution.
drills.french_formal: Ils n'ont pas eu de solution.
drills.french_street: Ils ont pas eu de solution.

drills.english: I had a lot of work.
drills.french_formal: J'avais beaucoup de travail.

drills.english: You had the time, no?
drills.french_formal: Tu avais le temps, non ?

drills.english: He had a plan.
drills.french_formal: Il avait un plan.

drills.english: We had no money at all.
drills.french_formal: Nous n'avions pas d'argent du tout.
drills.french_street: On avait pas d'argent du tout.

drills.english: I'm going to have a coffee.
drills.french_formal: Je vais avoir un café.

drills.english: You're going to have a problem.
drills.french_formal: Tu vas avoir un problème.

drills.english: We're going to have time, it's fine.
drills.french_formal: Nous allons avoir le temps, ce n'est pas grave.
drills.french_street: On va avoir le temps, c'est pas grave.

drills.english: They're going to have the solution.
drills.french_formal: Ils vont avoir la solution.

nodes.title: aller — to go
nodes.explanation: Aller is your MVP verb — you already know 'ça va' and 'on y va.' The present is dead simple: je vais, tu vas, il va, on va. Street French? Just squeeze it: j'vais. And the future? Stick any infinitive after it. You're already doing this.
nodes.street_notes: je vais → j'vais; ça va is the workhorse. Passé composé with être: je suis allé(e).

drills.english: I'm going to the café.
drills.french_formal: Je vais au café.

drills.english: Are you going to the metro?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu vas au métro ?

drills.english: He's not going to the restaurant.
drills.french_formal: Il ne va pas au restaurant.

drills.english: We're going to the beach.
drills.french_formal: Nous allons à la plage.

drills.english: Shall we go?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce qu'on y va ?

drills.english: They're going to Paris.
drills.french_formal: Ils vont à Paris.

drills.english: I went to Paris last week.
drills.french_formal: Je suis allé à Paris la semaine dernière.
drills.french_street: J'suis allé à Paris la semaine dernière.

drills.english: Did you go to the café?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu es allé au café ?

drills.french_formal: Il n'est pas allé — ce n'est pas possible.
drills.french_street: Il est pas allé — c'est pas possible.

drills.english: We went to the beach, it was great.
drills.french_formal: Nous sommes allés à la plage, c'était super.
drills.french_street: On est allés à la plage, c'était super.

drills.english: I used to go to work by metro.
drills.french_formal: J'allais au travail en métro.

drills.english: You were going to the café every morning.

drills.french_street: T'allais au café tous les matins.

drills.english: He was going to leave — it was rough.
drills.french_formal: Il allait partir — c'était difficile.

drills.english: We used to go to the beach when the weather was nice.
drills.french_formal: Nous allions à la plage quand il faisait beau.
drills.french_street: On allait à la plage quand il faisait beau.

drills.english: I'm going to eat.
drills.french_formal: Je vais manger.

drills.english: Are you going to come?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu vas venir ?

drills.french_formal: Donc nous allons voir — ça va marcher.
drills.french_street: Du coup on va voir — ça va marcher.

drills.english: They're going to pay — it's not a problem.
drills.french_formal: Ils vont payer — ce n'est pas grave.
drills.french_street: Ils vont payer — c'est pas grave.

nodes.title: faire — to do / to make
nodes.explanation: Faire means 'to do' or 'to make' — and it's everywhere. Most forms sound like 'fay': je fais, tu fais, il fait. The odd one out is ils font. On the street, je fais shrinks to j'fais. You'll use this verb every single day.
nodes.street_notes: je fais → j'fais; tu fais quoi ? is the default question form.

drills.english: I'm doing it.
drills.french_formal: Je le fais.

drills.english: What are you doing?
drills.french_formal: Tu fais quoi ?

drills.english: It's not possible, we can't do that.
drills.french_formal: Ce n'est pas possible, on ne peut pas faire ça.
drills.french_street: C'est pas possible, on peut pas faire ça.

drills.english: The weather's nice.
drills.french_formal: Il fait beau.

drills.english: He does it every day, it's normal.
drills.french_formal: Il le fait tous les jours, c'est normal.
drills.french_street: Il l'fait tous les jours, c'est normal.

drills.english: They do what they want.
drills.french_formal: Ils font ce qu'ils veulent.

drills.english: I made a coffee.
drills.french_formal: J'ai fait un café.

drills.english: Did you do it? — I have no idea.
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu as fait ça ? — Je ne sais pas.

drills.english: We made a plan, it's great.
drills.french_formal: On a fait un plan, c'est super.
drills.french_street: On a fait un plan, c'est super.

drills.english: They made a solution, totally.
drills.french_formal: Ils ont fait une solution, absolument.
drills.french_street: Ils ont fait une solution, carrément.

drills.english: I used to do that every morning.
drills.french_formal: Je faisais ça tous les matins.

drills.english: The weather was nice, so we went to the beach.
drills.french_formal: Il faisait beau, donc on est allés à la plage.
drills.french_street: Il faisait beau, du coup on est allés à la plage.

drills.english: You weren't doing anything, that's not normal.
drills.french_formal: Tu ne faisais rien, ce n'est pas normal.
drills.french_street: Tu faisais rien, c'est pas normal.

drills.english: What were you guys doing?
drills.french_formal: Qu'est-ce que vous faisiez ?

drills.english: I'm going to do it, no worries.
drills.french_formal: Je vais le faire, ce n'est pas grave.

drills.english: We're going to make a plan, shall we go?
drills.french_formal: On va faire un plan, on y va ?

drills.english: Are you going to do that? That's rough.
drills.french_formal: Tu vas faire ça ? C'est difficile.
drills.french_street: Tu vas faire ça ? C'est chaud.

drills.english: He's going to do it, I want to see that.
drills.french_formal: Il va le faire, je veux voir ça.
drills.french_street: Il va l'faire, j'veux voir ça.

nodes.title: vouloir — to want
nodes.explanation: Vouloir — 'to want' — sounds almost the same in je, tu, and il: veux, veux, veut. On the street, je veux becomes j'veux. The past is dead simple: voulu every time. And 'I wanted'? Je voulais — you'll hear it everywhere.
nodes.street_notes: je veux → j'veux; tu veux → tu veux (no contraction, consonant).

drills.english: I want a coffee.
drills.french_formal: Je veux un café.

drills.english: Do you want to eat?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu veux manger ?

drills.english: He wants to leave.
drills.french_formal: Il veut partir.

drills.english: We want a table.
drills.french_formal: Nous voulons une table.

drills.english: I don't want to take the metro.
drills.french_formal: Je ne veux pas prendre le métro.

drills.english: They want to go to Paris.
drills.french_formal: Ils veulent aller à Paris.

drills.english: I wanted a coffee, but it's not possible.
drills.french_formal: Je voulais un café, mais ce n'est pas possible.
drills.french_street: J'voulais un café, mais c'est pas possible.

drills.english: You wanted to speak French.
drills.french_formal: Tu voulais parler français.

drills.english: He wanted to pay, but it was complicated.
drills.french_formal: Il voulait payer, mais c'était compliqué.
drills.french_street: Il voulait payer, mais c'était compliqué.

drills.english: We wanted to go to the beach.
drills.french_formal: Nous voulions aller à la plage.
drills.french_street: On voulait aller à la plage.

drills.english: Did you want some? I had some.
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu as voulu en avoir ? J'en ai voulu.
drills.french_street: T'as voulu en vouloir ? J'en ai voulu.

drills.english: I wanted it — I asked for it.
drills.french_formal: Je l'ai voulu.

drills.english: He wanted to come but couldn't.
drills.french_formal: Il a voulu venir mais il n'a pas pu.
drills.french_street: Il a voulu venir mais il a pas pu.

drills.english: They wanted to see that.
drills.french_formal: Ils ont voulu voir ça.

drills.english: I'm going to want a coffee after the metro.
drills.french_formal: Je vais vouloir un café après le métro.
drills.french_street: J'vais vouloir un café après le métro.

drills.english: He's going to want to leave early.
drills.french_formal: Il va vouloir partir tôt.

drills.english: Are we going to want to pay for that? No way.
drills.french_formal: Est-ce qu'on va vouloir payer pour ça ? C'est n'importe quoi.
drills.french_street: On va vouloir payer pour ça ? N'importe quoi.

drills.english: You're going to want to see this — it's totally worth it.
drills.french_formal: Vous allez vouloir voir ça — c'est vraiment bien.
drills.french_street: Vous allez vouloir voir ça — carrément.

nodes.title: pouvoir — to be able to / can
nodes.explanation: Pouvoir means 'can' or 'to be able to' — and it's almost too easy. Tu peux, il peut, je peux — those three sound identical. On the street, je peux collapses to j'peux, and 'j'peux pas' is everywhere. The past? J'ai pu. Simple.
nodes.street_notes: je peux → j'peux; 'je peux pas' → 'j'peux pas' is one of the most common street strings.

drills.english: I can pay.
drills.french_formal: Je peux payer.

drills.english: I can't come.
drills.french_formal: Je ne peux pas venir.

drills.english: Can you help me?
drills.french_formal: Tu peux m'aider ?

drills.english: He can eat here.
drills.french_formal: Il peut manger ici.

drills.english: We can go to the café.
drills.french_formal: Nous pouvons aller au café.

drills.english: They can't do that — that's not normal!
drills.french_formal: Ils ne peuvent pas faire ça — ce n'est pas normal !
drills.french_street: Ils peuvent pas faire ça — c'est pas normal !

drills.english: I wasn't able to pay.
drills.french_formal: Je n'ai pas pu payer.

drills.english: Did you manage to come yesterday?
drills.french_formal: Tu as pu venir hier ?

drills.english: We managed to get a table.
drills.french_formal: Nous avons pu avoir une table.
drills.french_street: On a pu avoir une table.

drills.english: He couldn't finish.
drills.french_formal: Il n'a pas pu finir.

drills.english: Before, I could take the metro every day.
drills.french_formal: Avant, je pouvais prendre le métro tous les jours.
drills.french_street: Avant, j'pouvais prendre le métro tous les jours.

drills.english: You used to be able to eat there — it was great.
drills.french_formal: Tu pouvais manger là-bas — c'était super.
drills.french_street: Tu pouvais manger là-bas — c'était super.

drills.english: Back then we couldn't pay like that.
drills.french_formal: À l'époque, nous ne pouvions pas payer comme ça.
drills.french_street: À l'époque, on pouvait pas payer comme ça.

drills.english: It was rough — he couldn't leave.
drills.french_formal: C'était difficile — il ne pouvait pas partir.
drills.french_street: C'était chaud — il pouvait pas partir.

drills.english: I'm going to be able to come.
drills.french_formal: Je vais pouvoir venir.

drills.english: Are you going to be able to pay?
drills.french_formal: Tu vas pouvoir payer ?

drills.english: We're going to be able to sort it out.
drills.french_formal: Nous allons pouvoir trouver une solution.
drills.french_street: On va pouvoir trouver une solution.

nodes.title: devoir — to have to / must
nodes.explanation: Devoir — think 'duty', same root. You already know 'je dois partir' from day one. The whole present is basically 'dwa-dwa-dwa-devez-dwav' — three forms sound almost identical. On street, je dois just drops to j'dois. Honestly? You already own this verb.
nodes.street_notes: je dois → j'dois.

drills.english: I have to leave.
drills.french_formal: Je dois partir.

drills.english: You have to see that.
drills.french_formal: Tu dois voir ça.

drills.english: He has to pay.
drills.french_formal: Il doit payer.

drills.english: We have to take the métro.
drills.french_formal: Nous devons prendre le métro.

drills.english: I don't have to go.
drills.french_formal: Je ne dois pas y aller.

drills.english: They have to finish.
drills.french_formal: Ils doivent finir.

drills.english: I had to pay.
drills.french_formal: J'ai dû payer.

drills.english: Did you have to leave early?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu as dû partir tôt ?

drills.english: He had to work.
drills.french_formal: Il a dû travailler.

drills.english: We had to go to the café.
drills.french_formal: Nous avons dû aller au café.
drills.french_street: On a dû aller au café.

drills.english: I had to take the métro every day.
drills.french_formal: Je devais prendre le métro tous les jours.
drills.french_street: J'devais prendre le métro tous les jours.

drills.english: You had to speak French at work.
drills.french_formal: Tu devais parler français au travail.
drills.french_street: Tu devais parler français au travail.

drills.english: It wasn't possible — we had to wait.
drills.french_formal: Ce n'était pas possible — nous devions attendre.
drills.french_street: C'était pas possible — on devait attendre.

drills.english: They always had to pay.
drills.french_formal: Ils devaient toujours payer.

drills.english: I'm going to have to call him.
drills.french_formal: Je vais devoir l'appeler.

drills.english: You're going to have to see that — it's not normal.
drills.french_formal: Tu vas devoir voir ça — ce n'est pas normal.
drills.french_street: Tu vas devoir voir ça — c'est pas normal.

drills.english: We're going to have to find a solution.
drills.french_formal: Nous allons devoir trouver une solution.
drills.french_street: On va devoir trouver une solution.

drills.english: You're going to have to pay — that's how it is.
drills.french_formal: Vous allez devoir payer — c'est comme ça.
drills.french_street: Vous allez devoir payer — c'est comme ça.

nodes.title: savoir — to know (facts, how to)
nodes.explanation: Savoir means knowing a fact or knowing how to do something. The good news: je sais, tu sais, il sait — they all sound identical. And the negative? Je sais pas becomes j'sais pas becomes... chais pas. That's literally the name of this app. You already know the star form.
nodes.street_notes: je sais pas → j'sais pas → chais pas (the app's namesake).

drills.english: I don't know.
drills.french_formal: Je ne sais pas.

drills.english: Do you know where the metro is?
drills.french_formal: Tu sais où est le métro ?
drills.french_street: Tu sais où est le métro ?

drills.english: He knows it's not possible.
drills.french_formal: Il sait que ce n'est pas possible.
drills.french_street: Il sait que c'est pas possible.

drills.english: We don't know what we want.
drills.french_formal: On ne sait pas ce qu'on veut.
drills.french_street: On sait pas ce qu'on veut.

drills.english: I know how to speak French!
drills.french_formal: Je sais parler français !

drills.english: She doesn't know if it's going to work.
drills.french_formal: Elle ne sait pas si ça va marcher.
drills.french_street: Elle sait pas si ça va marcher.

drills.english: You already knew that, right?
drills.french_formal: Tu savais déjà ça, non ?

drills.english: I didn't know it was that rough.
drills.french_formal: Je ne savais pas que c'était aussi chaud.
drills.french_street: J'savais pas que c'était aussi chaud.

drills.english: We knew it wasn't possible.
drills.french_formal: On savait que ce n'était pas possible.
drills.french_street: On savait que c'était pas possible.

drills.english: He knew how to pay.
drills.french_formal: Il savait payer.

drills.english: Did you find out? (Did you come to know it?)
drills.french_formal: Tu as su ?

drills.english: I found out too late.
drills.french_formal: J'ai su trop tard.

drills.english: We found out it wasn't the solution.
drills.french_formal: On a su que ce n'était pas la solution.
drills.french_street: On a su que c'était pas la solution.

drills.english: They never found out.
drills.french_formal: Ils n'ont jamais su.

drills.english: You're going to know soon.
drills.french_formal: Tu vas savoir bientôt.

drills.english: I'm going to find out today.
drills.french_formal: Je vais savoir aujourd'hui.

drills.english: We're going to know if it works.
drills.french_formal: On va savoir si ça marche.
drills.french_street: On va savoir si ça marche.

drills.english: You're all going to know the solution.
drills.french_formal: Vous allez savoir la solution.

nodes.title: dire — to say / to tell
nodes.explanation: Dire means 'to say' or 'to tell' — and it sounds almost like 'dear' in English. The street shortcut is dead simple: je dis becomes j'dis. You'll hear on dit constantly — it means 'people say' or 'they say'. Past tense? J'ai dit. Done. Let's drill it.
nodes.street_notes: je dis → j'dis; on dit for general statements.

drills.english: I'm saying yes.
drills.french_formal: Je dis oui.

drills.english: What are you saying?
drills.french_formal: Qu'est-ce que tu dis ?

drills.english: They say it's not possible.
drills.french_formal: Ils disent que ce n'est pas possible.
drills.french_street: Ils disent que c'est pas possible.

drills.english: People say the weather's nice today.
drills.french_formal: On dit qu'il fait beau aujourd'hui.
drills.french_street: On dit que fait beau aujourd'hui.

drills.english: He's not saying anything.
drills.french_formal: Il ne dit rien.

drills.english: You tell me it's going to work — okay, I believe you.
drills.french_formal: Tu me dis que ça va marcher — d'accord.
drills.french_street: Tu m'dis que ça va marcher — ça marche.

drills.english: I told you so!
drills.french_formal: Je te l'ai dit !

drills.english: Did you say yes?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu as dit oui ?

drills.english: He said it's not serious.
drills.french_formal: Il a dit que ce n'est pas grave.
drills.french_street: Il a dit que c'est pas grave.

drills.english: We said we wanted to eat here.
drills.french_formal: On a dit qu'on voulait manger ici.
drills.french_street: On a dit qu'on voulait manger ici.

drills.english: I used to say I didn't know.
drills.french_formal: Je disais que je ne savais pas.

drills.english: She used to say it was great.
drills.french_formal: Elle disait que c'était super.

drills.english: You were saying you wanted to go to Paris.
drills.french_formal: Tu disais que tu voulais aller à Paris.
drills.french_street: Tu disais que tu voulais aller à Paris.

drills.english: They were all saying it was rough.
drills.french_formal: Ils disaient tous que c'était difficile.
drills.french_street: Ils disaient tous que c'était chaud.

drills.english: I'm going to tell him the solution.
drills.french_formal: Je vais lui dire la solution.

drills.english: Are you going to say something?
drills.french_formal: Tu vas dire quelque chose ?

drills.english: We're going to tell them it's not possible.
drills.french_formal: On va leur dire que ce n'est pas possible.
drills.french_street: On va leur dire que c'est pas possible.

drills.english: What are you going to tell him?
drills.french_formal: Qu'est-ce que vous allez lui dire ?

nodes.title: voir — to see
nodes.explanation: Voir — to see. In the present, you hear vois, voit, voient — they all sound the same. The past participle is vu, super short. On the street, je vois becomes j'vois, tu as vu becomes t'as vu. Think 'vision' — same root. Dead easy once you hear it.
nodes.street_notes: je vois → j'vois; tu vois as a filler ('you know').

drills.english: I see the problem.
drills.french_formal: Je vois le problème.

drills.english: Do you see what I mean?
drills.french_formal: Tu vois ce que je veux dire ?

drills.english: He sees her every day at the café.
drills.french_formal: Il la voit tous les jours au café.
drills.french_street: Il la voit tous les jours au café.

drills.english: We can't see anything — it's not possible.
drills.french_formal: On ne voit rien — ce n'est pas possible.
drills.french_street: On voit rien — c'est pas possible.

drills.english: You know, I don't see the solution.
drills.french_formal: Tu vois, je ne vois pas la solution.
drills.french_street: Tu vois, j'vois pas la solution.

drills.english: You see them at the métro, right?
drills.french_formal: Vous les voyez au métro, non ?
drills.french_street: Vous les voyez au métro, non ?

drills.english: I saw him yesterday, it's been a long time.
drills.french_formal: Je l'ai vu hier, ça fait longtemps.
drills.french_street: J'l'ai vu hier, ça fait longtemps.

drills.english: Did you see that? That's not normal.
drills.french_formal: Tu as vu ça ? Ce n'est pas normal.
drills.french_street: T'as vu ça ? C'est pas normal.

drills.english: We saw a great film.
drills.french_formal: On a vu un super film.
drills.french_street: On a vu un super film.

drills.english: They saw nothing — seriously, that's rough.
drills.french_formal: Ils n'ont rien vu — franchement, c'est difficile.
drills.french_street: Ils ont rien vu — carrément, c'est chaud.

drills.english: I used to see her every morning at the café.
drills.french_formal: Je la voyais tous les matins au café.
drills.french_street: J'la voyais tous les matins au café.

drills.english: You could see the problem — it was important.
drills.french_formal: Tu voyais le problème — c'était important.
drills.french_street: Tu voyais le problème — c'était important.

drills.english: We used to see them all the time, you know.
drills.french_formal: On les voyait tout le temps, tu vois.
drills.french_street: On les voyait tout le temps, tu vois.

drills.english: Back then, he saw her every week.
drills.french_formal: À l'époque, il la voyait toutes les semaines.
drills.french_street: À l'époque, il la voyait toutes les semaines.

drills.english: I'm going to see him tomorrow — sorted.
drills.french_formal: Je vais le voir demain — c'est bon.
drills.french_street: J'vais le voir demain — ça marche.

drills.english: We'll see — it's not certain.
drills.french_formal: On va voir — ce n'est pas certain.
drills.french_street: On va voir — c'est pas certain.

drills.english: You have to see this, you're going to love it.
drills.french_formal: Tu dois voir ça, tu vas adorer.
drills.french_street: Tu dois voir ça, tu vas adorer.

drills.english: They're going to see us at the métro.
drills.french_formal: Ils vont nous voir au métro.
drills.french_street: Ils vont nous voir au métro.

nodes.title: prendre — to take
nodes.explanation: Prendre — to take. You already know the sound: 'prends' like 'pron'. In the café it's your go-to: j'prends un café. The past is easy: j'ai pris. Think 'prize' — you took the prize. Street tip: je prends just squishes to j'prends. Dead simple.
nodes.street_notes: je prends → j'prends; the café-order verb: je prends un café.

drills.english: I'll have a coffee.
drills.french_formal: Je prends un café.

drills.english: Are you taking the metro?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu prends le métro ?

drills.english: He's taking the solution.
drills.french_formal: Il prend la solution.

drills.english: We're taking a coffee, obviously.
drills.french_formal: On prend un café, carrément.

drills.english: I'm not taking the metro today.
drills.french_formal: Je ne prends pas le métro aujourd'hui.

drills.english: They're taking it — there's no other solution.
drills.french_formal: Ils le prennent — il n'y a pas d'autre solution.
drills.french_street: Ils le prennent — y'a pas d'autre solution.

drills.english: I took a coffee this morning.
drills.french_formal: J'ai pris un café ce matin.
drills.french_street: J'ai pris un café ce matin.

drills.english: Did you take the metro?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu as pris le métro ?

drills.english: He took it — I saw him.
drills.french_formal: Il l'a pris — je l'ai vu.

drills.english: We took the wrong one — that's not normal.
drills.french_formal: On a pris le mauvais — ce n'est pas normal.
drills.french_street: On a pris le mauvais — c'est pas normal.

drills.english: I used to take the metro every day.
drills.french_formal: Je prenais le métro tous les jours.
drills.french_street: J'prenais le métro tous les jours.

drills.english: Back then you used to take a coffee every morning.
drills.french_formal: Tu prenais un café chaque matin à l'époque.
drills.french_street: Tu prenais un café chaque matin à l'époque.

drills.english: He used to take it — it was great.
drills.french_formal: Il le prenait — c'était super.

drills.english: We used to take the bus, so it was fine.
drills.french_formal: On prenait le bus, du coup c'était possible.
drills.french_street: On prenait le bus, du coup c'était possible.

drills.english: I'm going to take a coffee.
drills.french_formal: Je vais prendre un café.

drills.english: Are you going to take the metro or not?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu vas prendre le métro ou pas ?
drills.french_street: Tu vas prendre le métro ou pas ?

drills.english: We're going to take it — it's the best solution.
drills.french_formal: On va le prendre — c'est la meilleure solution.
drills.french_street: On va le prendre — c'est la meilleure solution.

drills.english: They're going to take it, totally.
drills.french_formal: Ils vont le prendre, carrément.

nodes.title: venir — to come
nodes.explanation: Venir — 'to come' — sounds like 'venue', the place you come to. The big news: it takes être in the passé composé, just like aller. And in the street, je viens becomes j'viens in a heartbeat. Once you hear that, the whole family clicks.
nodes.street_notes: je viens → j'viens; tu viens ? as a one-word invitation. Passé composé with être.

drills.english: I'm coming!
drills.french_formal: Je viens !

drills.english: Are you coming to the café?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu viens au café ?

drills.english: He's not coming — that's not normal.
drills.french_formal: Il ne vient pas — ce n'est pas normal.
drills.french_street: Il vient pas — c'est pas normal.

drills.english: We're coming with you.
drills.french_formal: Nous venons avec toi.

drills.english: Can you come? It's important.
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu peux venir ? C'est important.

drills.english: They're coming — great!
drills.french_formal: Ils viennent — c'est super.

drills.english: I came by metro.
drills.french_formal: Je suis venu en métro.

drills.english: Did you come to the party?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu es venu à la fête ?

drills.english: He came — I didn't see him.
drills.french_formal: Il est venu — je ne l'ai pas vu.
drills.french_street: Il est venu — j'l'ai pas vu.

drills.english: We came together — it was great.
drills.french_formal: Nous sommes venus ensemble — c'était super.
drills.french_street: On est venus ensemble — c'était super.

drills.english: I used to come here every day.
drills.french_formal: Je venais ici tous les jours.

drills.english: He used to come by — it was nice.
drills.french_formal: Il venait souvent — c'était bien.

drills.english: You used to come to work by metro?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu venais au travail en métro ?
drills.french_street: Tu venais au taf en métro ?

drills.english: They used to come — I don't know why they stopped.
drills.french_formal: Ils venaient — je ne sais pas pourquoi ils ont arrêté.
drills.french_street: Ils venaient — chais pas pourquoi ils ont arrêté.

drills.english: I'm going to come tomorrow.
drills.french_formal: Je vais venir demain.

drills.english: Are you going to come with us?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu vas venir avec nous ?

drills.english: He's going to come — that's great.
drills.french_formal: Il va venir — c'est super.

drills.english: We're going to come — see you soon!
drills.french_formal: Nous allons venir — à toute !

nodes.title: mettre — to put / to put on
nodes.explanation: Mettre — to put, or to put on. In everyday speech you'll hear j'mets, tu mets, il met — the t is totally silent. Think of it like 'commit': you met a deadline, you mis something down. One irregular past participle: mis. Street French just clips the je — j'mets mon manteau, done.
nodes.street_notes: je mets → j'mets.

drills.english: I'm putting my jacket on.
drills.french_formal: Je mets ma veste.

drills.english: You're putting your phone on the table.
drills.french_formal: Tu mets ton téléphone sur la table.
drills.french_street: Tu mets ton téléphone sur la table.

drills.english: He's putting sugar in his coffee.
drills.french_formal: Il met du sucre dans son café.
drills.french_street: Il met du sucre dans son café.

drills.english: We're putting our stuff down here.
drills.french_formal: On met nos affaires ici.

drills.english: I'm not putting my coat on — it's warm.
drills.french_formal: Je ne mets pas mon manteau — il fait chaud.
drills.french_street: J'mets pas mon manteau — fait chaud.

drills.english: They're putting their bags on the metro.
drills.french_formal: Ils mettent leurs sacs dans le métro.
drills.french_street: Ils mettent leurs sacs dans le métro.

drills.english: I put my keys somewhere — no idea where.
drills.french_formal: J'ai mis mes clés quelque part — je ne sais pas où.
drills.french_street: J'ai mis mes clés quelque part — chais pas où.

drills.english: Did you put the solution in the report?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu as mis la solution dans le rapport ?
drills.french_street: T'as mis la solution dans le rapport ?

drills.english: He put his phone on the table, just like that.
drills.french_formal: Il a mis son téléphone sur la table, comme ça.
drills.french_street: Il a mis son téléphone sur la table, comme ça.

drills.english: We put our stuff there — it's fine.
drills.french_formal: On a mis nos affaires là — c'est bon.
drills.french_street: On a mis nos affaires là — c'est bon.

drills.english: Back then I always used to put sugar in my coffee.
drills.french_formal: Avant, je mettais toujours du sucre dans mon café.
drills.french_street: Avant, j'mettais toujours du sucre dans mon café.

drills.english: You used to put your bag on the seat, remember?
drills.french_formal: Tu mettais ton sac sur le siège, tu te souviens ?
drills.french_street: Tu mettais ton sac sur le siège, tu t'souviens ?

drills.english: He was always putting his work off — it was rough.
drills.french_formal: Il mettait toujours son travail de côté — c'était chaud.
drills.french_street: Il mettait toujours son travail de côté — c'était chaud.

drills.english: We used to put everything on the table like that.
drills.french_formal: On mettait tout sur la table comme ça.
drills.french_street: On mettait tout sur la table comme ça.

drills.english: I'm going to put my jacket on — it's cold.
drills.french_formal: Je vais mettre ma veste — il fait froid.
drills.french_street: J'vais mettre ma veste — fait froid.

drills.english: Are you going to put it there or not?
drills.french_formal: Tu vas le mettre là ou pas ?
drills.french_street: Tu vas l'mettre là ou pas ?

drills.english: We're going to put the solution on the table — that's it.
drills.french_formal: On va mettre la solution sur la table — voilà, c'est tout.
drills.french_street: On va mettre la solution sur la table — voilà, c'est tout.

drills.english: Where are you going to put all this stuff?
drills.french_formal: Vous allez mettre tout ça où ?
drills.french_street: Vous allez mettre tout ça où ?

nodes.title: parler — to speak / to talk
nodes.explanation: Parler is your model -er verb — and the best news? Je parle, il parle, ils parlent all sound exactly the same. One sound, three people. Add t'as parlé for the past, j'vais parler for near future — and you're already speaking like a Parisian.
nodes.street_notes: The model -er verb: one spoken form for je/tu/il/on/ils (parle/parles/parlent all sound identical).

drills.english: I speak French.
drills.french_formal: Je parle français.

drills.english: You're talking to him?
drills.french_formal: Tu lui parles ?

drills.english: She's talking on the phone.
drills.french_formal: Elle parle au téléphone.

drills.english: We're talking about the situation.
drills.french_formal: Nous parlons de la situation.

drills.english: I'm not talking to you.
drills.french_formal: Je ne te parle pas.

drills.english: They're all talking at the same time — that's not normal!
drills.french_formal: Ils parlent tous en même temps — ce n'est pas normal !
drills.french_street: Ils parlent tous en même temps — c'est pas normal !

drills.english: Did you talk to him?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu as parlé avec lui ?

drills.english: I talked to her at the café.
drills.french_formal: J'ai parlé avec elle au café.
drills.french_street: J'ai parlé avec elle au café.

drills.english: We talked about the solution.
drills.french_formal: Nous avons parlé de la solution.
drills.french_street: On a parlé de la solution.

drills.english: They talked for a long time.
drills.french_formal: Ils ont parlé longtemps.

drills.english: I used to talk to him every day on the métro.
drills.french_formal: Je lui parlais tous les jours dans le métro.
drills.french_street: J'lui parlais tous les jours dans le métro.

drills.english: She was talking — it wasn't possible to stop her.
drills.french_formal: Elle parlait — ce n'était pas possible de l'arrêter.
drills.french_street: Elle parlait — c'était pas possible de l'arrêter.

drills.english: We were talking about you.
drills.french_formal: Nous parlions de toi.

drills.english: You were always talking, well yeah, that's how it was.
drills.french_formal: Tu parlais tout le temps, eh bien, c'était comme ça.
drills.french_street: Tu parlais tout le temps, bah ouais, c'était comme ça.

drills.english: I'm going to talk to him about it.
drills.french_formal: Je vais lui parler.

drills.english: Are you going to talk to the boss?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu vas parler au responsable ?

drills.english: We're going to talk about it — it's important.
drills.french_formal: Nous allons en parler — c'est important.
drills.french_street: On va en parler — c'est important.

drills.english: You're all going to talk? That's going to take a while.
drills.french_formal: Vous allez parler ? Ça va prendre du temps.
drills.french_street: Vous allez parler ? Ça va prendre du temps.

nodes.title: aimer — to like / to love
nodes.explanation: Aimer — to like or love. It's almost English: 'I aim to love it.' The endings melt away in speech — j'aime, il aime, ils aiment all sound identical. Slap t'aimes for tu, and you're done. Chuck j'aime bien out there and you're instantly likeable.
nodes.street_notes: j'aime is elision, not slang — it's the only correct form. j'aime bien = I like (softer than j'aime).

drills.english: I like the metro.
drills.french_formal: J'aime le métro.

drills.english: Do you like coffee?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu aimes le café ?

drills.english: He likes that. Totally.
drills.french_formal: Il aime ça. Tout à fait.

drills.english: We like eating here.
drills.french_formal: Nous aimons manger ici.

drills.english: I don't like the situation.
drills.french_formal: Je n'aime pas la situation.

drills.english: You like it, don't you?
drills.french_formal: Tu aimes ça, non ?

drills.english: I liked the coffee.
drills.french_formal: J'ai aimé le café.

drills.english: Did you like the film?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu as aimé le film ?

drills.english: We really liked that.
drills.french_formal: On a vraiment aimé ça.

drills.english: They liked the solution.
drills.french_formal: Ils ont aimé la solution.

drills.english: I used to like taking the metro.
drills.french_formal: J'aimais prendre le métro.

drills.english: You used to like it, right?
drills.french_formal: Tu aimais ça, non ?

drills.english: He liked her — it was complicated.
drills.french_formal: Il l'aimait — c'était compliqué.

drills.english: We used to like going out. It was great.
drills.french_formal: On aimait sortir. C'était super.

drills.english: I'm going to like this.
drills.french_formal: Je vais aimer ça.

drills.english: Are you going to like working there?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu vas aimer travailler là-bas ?

drills.english: They're going to love it. It's going to work.
drills.french_formal: Ils vont aimer ça. Ça va marcher.
drills.french_street: Ils vont aimer ça. Ça va marcher.

drills.english: You're all going to like this — it's possible.
drills.french_formal: Vous allez aimer ça — c'est possible.
drills.french_street: Vous allez aimer ça — c'est possible.

nodes.title: acheter — to buy
nodes.explanation: Acheter — to buy. It's almost 'to acquire', dead easy to remember. Spell it with a plain E in the middle, but hear that grave-accent È whenever the ending is silent: j'achète, il achète. Street: t'achètes, j'vais acheter. The passé composé is just j'ai acheté — totally regular.
nodes.street_notes: Stem-change -er verb: è in the singular/ils forms (j'achète), plain e when the ending is pronounced (vous achetez).

drills.english: I'm buying a coffee.
drills.french_formal: J'achète un café.

drills.english: Are you buying that?
drills.french_formal: Tu achètes ça ?

drills.english: He's buying a metro ticket.
drills.french_formal: Il achète un ticket de métro.
drills.french_street: Il achète un ticket de métro.

drills.english: We're buying something to eat.
drills.french_formal: On achète quelque chose à manger.
drills.french_street: On achète quelque chose à manger.

drills.english: I don't want to buy that.
drills.french_formal: Je ne veux pas acheter ça.

drills.english: They're buying everything.
drills.french_formal: Ils achètent tout.

drills.english: I bought a coffee this morning.
drills.french_formal: J'ai acheté un café ce matin.
drills.french_street: J'ai acheté un café ce matin.

drills.english: Did you buy it?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu l'as acheté ?

drills.english: We bought metro tickets.
drills.french_formal: On a acheté des tickets de métro.
drills.french_street: On a acheté des tickets de métro.

drills.english: He didn't buy anything.
drills.french_formal: Il n'a rien acheté.

drills.english: I used to buy coffee there every morning.
drills.french_formal: J'achetais un café là-bas tous les matins.
drills.french_street: J'achetais un café là-bas tous les matins.

drills.english: You were buying stuff at the market.
drills.french_formal: Tu achetais des trucs au marché.

drills.english: We used to buy everything here.
drills.french_formal: On achetait tout ici.

drills.english: They were buying tickets when I arrived.
drills.french_formal: Ils achetaient des billets quand je suis arrivé.
drills.french_street: Ils achetaient des billets quand j'suis arrivé.

drills.english: I'm going to buy a coffee.
drills.french_formal: Je vais acheter un café.

drills.english: Are you going to buy that?
drills.french_formal: Tu vas acheter ça ?

drills.english: We're going to buy tickets for the film.
drills.french_formal: On va acheter des billets pour le film.
drills.french_street: On va acheter des billets pour le film.

drills.english: I'd like to buy this — it's possible, right?
drills.french_formal: Je voudrais acheter ça — c'est possible, non ?
drills.french_street: J'voudrais acheter ça — c'est possible, non ?

nodes.title: manger — to eat
nodes.explanation: Manger — to eat. Most forms sound identical: je mange, tu manges, il mange — all pronounced the same. Street tip: on mange? with a little rise is your instant invitation to eat. And watch the imparfait: it keeps that soft e — je mangeais. You already know j'veux manger, so this whole verb is yours.
nodes.street_notes: on mange ? as an invitation. Imparfait keeps the e: je mangeais.

drills.english: I'm eating a croissant.
drills.french_formal: Je mange un croissant.

drills.english: Are you eating here?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu manges ici ?

drills.english: He's eating at the restaurant.
drills.french_formal: Il mange au restaurant.

drills.english: Shall we eat?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce qu'on mange ?

drills.english: I don't eat at the office.
drills.french_formal: Je ne mange pas au bureau.

drills.english: They're eating, so we can go.
drills.french_formal: Ils mangent, donc on peut partir.
drills.french_street: Ils mangent, du coup on peut partir.

drills.english: I ate at the café.
drills.french_formal: J'ai mangé au café.

drills.english: Did you eat? I didn't see you.
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu as mangé ? Je ne t'ai pas vu.

drills.english: We ate on the metro, totally.
drills.french_formal: Nous avons mangé dans le métro, absolument.
drills.french_street: On a mangé dans le métro, carrément.

drills.english: They ate already — it's not possible.
drills.french_formal: Ils ont mangé déjà — ce n'est pas possible.
drills.french_street: Ils ont déjà mangé — c'est pas possible.

drills.english: I used to eat here every day.
drills.french_formal: Je mangeais ici tous les jours.

drills.english: You used to eat a croissant every morning.
drills.french_formal: Tu mangeais un croissant tous les matins.
drills.french_street: Tu mangeais un croissant tous les matins.

drills.english: It was cold out and we were eating outside — that was rough.
drills.french_formal: Il faisait froid et nous mangions dehors — c'était difficile.
drills.french_street: Fait froid et on mangeait dehors — c'était chaud.

drills.english: They were eating when I arrived.
drills.french_formal: Ils mangeaient quand je suis arrivé.

drills.english: I'm going to eat a pizza.
drills.french_formal: Je vais manger une pizza.

drills.english: Are you going to eat with us?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu vas manger avec nous ?

drills.english: We're going to eat there — it's going to be great.
drills.french_formal: Nous allons manger là-bas — ça va être super.
drills.french_street: On va manger là-bas — ça va être super.

drills.english: You (formal) are going to eat well here.
drills.french_formal: Vous allez manger bien ici.

nodes.title: partir — to leave
nodes.explanation: Partir means 'to leave' or 'to go'. It sounds like 'departure' — so you already know it. The je and tu forms drop the 't': je pars, tu pars. On the street, je pars becomes j'pars. And it takes être in the passé composé — just like aller.
nodes.street_notes: je pars → j'pars; on part ? = shall we go? Passé composé with être: je suis parti(e).

drills.english: I'm leaving now.
drills.french_formal: Je pars maintenant.

drills.english: Are you leaving already?
drills.french_formal: Tu pars déjà ?

drills.english: He's leaving for Paris.
drills.french_formal: Il part pour Paris.

drills.english: Shall we go? It's not possible to wait.
drills.french_formal: On part ? Ce n'est pas possible d'attendre.
drills.french_street: On part ? C'est pas possible d'attendre.

drills.english: I don't want to leave.
drills.french_formal: Je ne veux pas partir.

drills.english: You have to leave, it's important.
drills.french_formal: Tu dois partir, c'est important.

drills.english: I left at seven.
drills.french_formal: Je suis parti à sept heures.

drills.english: Did you leave without me?
drills.french_formal: Tu es parti sans moi ?

drills.english: We left for the beach.
drills.french_formal: Nous sommes partis à la plage.
drills.french_street: On est partis à la plage.

drills.english: They left already — that's rough.
drills.french_formal: Ils sont partis déjà — c'est difficile.
drills.french_street: Ils sont partis déjà — c'est chaud.

drills.english: I used to leave early every day.
drills.french_formal: Je partais tôt tous les jours.

drills.english: He was leaving when I arrived.
drills.french_formal: Il partait quand je suis arrivé.

drills.english: We used to leave by métro.
drills.french_formal: Nous partions en métro.

drills.english: You used to leave so late — it wasn't normal.
drills.french_formal: Tu partais si tard — ce n'était pas normal.
drills.french_street: Tu partais si tard — c'était pas normal.

drills.english: I'm going to leave in five minutes.
drills.french_formal: Je vais partir dans cinq minutes.

drills.english: Are you going to leave soon?
drills.french_formal: Tu vas partir bientôt ?

drills.english: We're going to leave — shall we go?
drills.french_formal: Nous allons partir — on y va ?
drills.french_street: On va partir — on y va ?

drills.english: They're going to leave without a solution.
drills.french_formal: Ils vont partir sans solution.

nodes.title: finir — to finish
nodes.explanation: Finir is your model -ir verb — think 'finish', totally transparent. Singular forms sound the same: je finis, tu finis, il finit. Then -iss- kicks in for the rest. Passé composé? Just j'ai fini — 'I'm done.' Street gold: t'as fini? means 'you done?'
nodes.street_notes: The model regular -ir verb (-iss- in plural forms). c'est fini = it's over.

drills.english: I'm finishing my coffee.
drills.french_formal: Je finis mon café.

drills.english: Are you finishing soon?
drills.french_formal: Tu finis bientôt ?

drills.english: He's finishing work.
drills.french_formal: Il finit le travail.

drills.english: We're not finishing yet.
drills.french_formal: On ne finit pas encore.

drills.english: You guys finish at what time?
drills.french_formal: Vous finissez à quelle heure ?

drills.english: They finish late.
drills.french_formal: Ils finissent tard.

drills.english: Did you finish?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu as fini ?

drills.english: I finished, it's over.
drills.french_formal: J'ai fini, c'est fini.

drills.english: We finished late, so we went.
drills.french_formal: On a fini tard, du coup on est partis.
drills.french_street: On a fini tard, du coup on est partis.

drills.english: They finished the coffee.
drills.french_formal: Ils ont fini le café.

drills.english: I used to finish early.
drills.french_formal: Je finissais tôt.

drills.english: You were always finishing late.
drills.french_formal: Tu finissais toujours tard.

drills.english: He was finishing when I arrived.
drills.french_formal: Il finissait quand je suis arrivé.

drills.english: We used to finish at the café.
drills.french_formal: On finissait au café.

drills.english: I'm going to finish this.
drills.french_formal: Je vais finir ça.

drills.english: Are you going to finish before the métro?
drills.french_formal: Tu vas finir avant le métro ?
drills.french_street: Tu vas finir avant le métro ?

drills.english: It's not going to finish, it's ridiculous.
drills.french_formal: Ça ne va pas finir, c'est n'importe quoi.
drills.french_street: Ça va pas finir, c'est n'importe quoi.

drills.english: We're going to finish together, that works.
drills.french_formal: On va finir ensemble, ça marche.
drills.french_street: On va finir ensemble, ça marche.

nodes.title: donner — to give
nodes.explanation: Donner — 'to give' — is a textbook regular -er verb, so once you've got it, you've got hundreds. The present sounds almost the same across je, tu, il — just donne, donne, donne. Street tip: donne-moi means 'give me', and ça donne quoi is your new go-to for 'what's it like?'
nodes.street_notes: donne-moi = give me (imperative preview); ça donne quoi ? = how's it going / what's it like?

drills.english: I'm giving you a coffee.
drills.french_formal: Je te donne un café.

drills.english: She gives me her number.
drills.french_formal: Elle me donne son numéro.

drills.english: What's it like? — I don't know.
drills.french_formal: Ça donne quoi ? Je ne sais pas.

drills.english: We're not giving them the solution.
drills.french_formal: On ne leur donne pas la solution.
drills.french_street: On leur donne pas la solution.

drills.english: Do you want to give me a hand?
drills.french_formal: Tu veux me donner un coup de main ?
drills.french_street: Tu veux me donner un coup de main ?

drills.english: It's not possible — he's giving nothing.
drills.french_formal: Ce n'est pas possible — il ne donne rien.
drills.french_street: C'est pas possible — il donne rien.

drills.english: Did you give him the address?
drills.french_formal: Est-ce que tu lui as donné l'adresse ?

drills.english: I gave you my number, remember?
drills.french_formal: Je t'ai donné mon numéro, tu t'en souviens ?
drills.french_street: J't'ai donné mon numéro, tu t'souviens ?

drills.english: They gave us the wrong solution.
drills.french_formal: Ils nous ont donné la mauvaise solution.
drills.french_street: Ils nous ont donné la mauvaise solution.

drills.english: We gave everything — it was rough.
drills.french_formal: On a tout donné — c'était difficile.
drills.french_street: On a tout donné — c'était chaud.

drills.english: He used to give me a coffee every morning.
drills.french_formal: Il me donnait un café chaque matin.
drills.french_street: Il me donnait un café chaque matin.

drills.english: Before, we used to give them time.
drills.french_formal: Avant, on leur donnait du temps.
drills.french_street: Avant, on leur donnait du temps.

drills.english: You were giving it everything — I saw that.
drills.french_formal: Tu donnais tout — j'ai vu ça.
drills.french_street: Tu donnais tout — j'ai vu ça.

drills.english: Back then, I used to give you my metro card.
drills.french_formal: À l'époque, je te donnais mon passe Navigo.
drills.french_street: À l'époque, j'te donnais mon passe Navigo.

drills.english: I'm going to give you the address.
drills.french_formal: Je vais te donner l'adresse.

drills.english: Are you going to give it to him?
drills.french_formal: Tu vas le lui donner ?

drills.english: So we're going to give them one more chance.
drills.french_formal: Donc on va leur donner une autre chance.
drills.french_street: Du coup on va leur donner une autre chance.

drills.english: You all are going to give me the solution, right?
drills.french_formal: Vous allez me donner la solution, non ?
drills.french_street: Vous allez me donner la solution, non ?

nodes.title: Politeness conditionals: je voudrais, je pourrais, il faudrait
nodes.explanation: Six magic fixed forms — je voudrais, j'aimerais, je pourrais, tu pourrais, vous pourriez, il faudrait, ce serait — no conjugation, just drop them in front of any request and every shop, café, and hotel interaction instantly becomes smoother and more natural.

drills.english: I'd like a coffee.
drills.french_formal: Je voudrais un café.

drills.english: I'd like to eat something.
drills.french_formal: Je voudrais manger quelque chose.

drills.english: I would love a table for two.
drills.french_formal: J'aimerais une table pour deux.

drills.english: I would love to go to the restaurant.
drills.french_formal: J'aimerais aller au restaurant.

drills.english: Could I pay now?
drills.french_formal: Je pourrais payer maintenant ?

drills.english: Could I have the one I saw?
drills.french_formal: Je pourrais avoir celui que j'ai vu ?

drills.english: Could you help me? (informal)
drills.french_formal: Tu pourrais m'aider ?

drills.english: Could you call me back? (informal)
drills.french_formal: Tu pourrais me rappeler ?

drills.english: Could you help me? (formal)
drills.french_formal: Vous pourriez m'aider ?

drills.english: Could you tell me where to go? (formal)
drills.french_formal: Vous pourriez me dire où aller ?
drills.french_street: Vous pourriez me dire où aller ?

drills.english: We'd need to book a table.
drills.french_formal: Il faudrait réserver une table.

drills.english: We'd need to leave soon.
drills.french_formal: Il faudrait partir bientôt.

drills.english: That would be great.
drills.french_formal: Ce serait super.

drills.english: It would be possible to eat here.
drills.french_formal: Ce serait possible de manger ici.
drills.french_street: Ça serait possible de manger ici.

drills.english: I'd like the one I ordered.
drills.french_formal: Je voudrais celui que j'ai commandé.

drills.english: It would be a good solution.
drills.french_formal: Ce serait une bonne solution.
